label: README — How to run
info:
# Example 14 — Full Suite Validation

Self-asserting validation flow exercising EVERY node in node-red-contrib-opcua-suite.
Each tab ends in an assert Function node that emits a `[VALIDATE] <id>: PASS` (or FAIL) warning.

## How to run
1. Deploy this flow (full deploy).
2. Wait ~3s for the shared OPC UA sessions to connect.
3. Click every inject node (or the per-tab trigger).
4. Watch the debug sidebar / node-red log for `[VALIDATE] ... PASS` lines.

## Tabs
- C1 client READ — read ns=1;s=Scalar.Double ≈ 2.718281828
- C2 client WRITE+READBACK — write 42.5 to Writable.SetPoint, read back
- C3 client SUBSCRIBE — subscribe Dynamic.Sinus, PASS on first value
- C4 client BROWSE — browse TestData, assert children
- C5 client METHOD — Methods.Add(3,4)=7
- C6 client GETENDPOINTS — assert >=1 endpoint
- C7 client READMULTIPLE — Scalar.Double + Scalar.Int32 in one batch
- I1 opcua-item COLLECTOR — 2 item nodes -> client readmultiple
- B1 opcua-browser — browse TestData
- BC1 opcua-browse-client — loaded (editor-driven selection, see note)
- M1 opcua-method — Methods.Multiply(6,7)=42
- E1 opcua-event — subscribe (server raises no events, honest PASS)
- S1 opcua-server EMBEDDED — start server, add var, read back via client
- AUTH1 endpoint USERNAME/PASSWORD — admin/admin123 authenticated read
- T1..T9 — PubSub validation tabs carried over from example 13 (already pass live)

Endpoint (inside node-red container): opc.tcp://opcua-server:4840/UA/TestServer (security None/None)
Auth user: admin / admin123  |  MQTT: mqtt://val-mosquitto:1883

name: Example 14 — Full Suite Validation. See tab Info panel for run instructions.
assert C1
C2 — write 42.5 to Writable.SetPoint then read back
prep readback
func: return { topic: 'ns=1;s=Writable.SetPoint', operation: 'read' };

name: assert C2
C3 — subscribe Dynamic.Sinus; PASS on first value-change msg
assert C3
C4 — browse ns=1;s=TestData, assert child browseNames
assert C4
assert C5
assert C6
C7 — readmultiple Scalar.Double + Scalar.Int32 via msg.items
assert C7
assert I1
B1 — opcua-browser node browse TestData, assert child refs
assert B1
BC1 — browse-client runtime read of pre-selected items
assert BC1
assert M1
E1 — subscribe events on Server node (i=2253). Test server raises none -> honest PASS on subscribe ack.
assert E1
S1 — embedded server on :48400, add var + set value, client reads it back
assert S1
AUTH1 — endpoint with admin/admin123 credentials, assert authenticated read
assert AUTH1
T1 — UDP-UADP round-trip of all scalar types (Double/String/Int32/Boolean/Float/UInt16); assert values + encoding/transport/headers
assert
T2 — MQTT-UADP round-trip; assert fields + encoding=uadp, transport=mqtt, topic present
assert
T3 — MQTT-JSON; assert encoding=json, transport=mqtt, writerGroupId DEFINED (CR-02), timestamp=publish time (HI-01), numeric publisherId stays number (HI-02)
assert
T4 — one publisher with TWO DataSetWriters (id 1 & 2); two subscribers each filter their own dataSetWriterId and must see only their dataset fields
assertA
assertB
T5 — cyclic publish (600ms). First inject -> keyframe (non-empty payload). With no new input a later KeepAlive arrives (empty payload). Assert both.
reset+setVal
classify
assert
T6 — small MTU (200) + ~2000-char string over UDP-UADP forces multi-chunk; subscriber must reassemble the FULL string intact
assert
T7 — publisherIdType UInt32, numeric id 305419896 over UDP-UADP; assert msg.publisherId === 305419896 (number)
assert
T8 — publisher writerGroupId 7; subA filters wg7 (must receive), subB filters wg999 (must receive NOTHING). subB-path sets flag; delayed check PASSes if no msg.
reset flags
markA
func: flow.set("t8_a", true); return null;

name: markB
func: flow.set("t8_b", true); return null;

name: assert
T9 — publisher PDS configurationVersion {1,0}; subscriber expectedConfigVersion 2.0. Subscriber must NOT emit a normal msg (logs node.error). Delayed check PASSes if nothing arrived.
reset flag
func: flow.set("t9_recv", false); return null;

name: mark recv
func: flow.set("t9_recv", true); return null;

name: assert
